behaviors.description: When a required key is absent from both file and environment, the CLI error names the missing key with its exact config path (for example `database.host`).
behaviors.justification: A real failure is usually in wiring between YAML load, env overlay, required-key validation, and error formatting. A pure unit test of only the validator can miss path-construction mismatches introduced by loader integration.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real config loader against a temp YAML file that omits `database.host`, with `TOOL_DATABASE_HOST` unset. Assert startup fails and stderr includes the exact path string `database.host` in the actionable error.

behaviors.description: When `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` is set, config is resolved from that directory instead of `~/.config`.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real env-var reading plus path resolution in loader wiring. A mock of path/env helpers can hide incorrect precedence or fallback behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Set `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` to a temp directory containing `tool/config.yaml`; also place a different config under `~/.config/tool/config.yaml`. Run loader and assert values come from the XDG location.

behaviors.description: When a `TOOL_` environment variable conflicts with YAML config, the environment value wins.
behaviors.justification: The real risk is precedence logic across parsed file config and env override mapping. Unit tests on merge helpers alone can miss naming/normalization mismatches in end-to-end loader wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Create YAML with `database.host: file-host`; set `TOOL_DATABASE_HOST=env-host`; execute loader and assert effective config uses `env-host`.

behaviors.description: When YAML has a syntax error, the error message includes the parser-reported line number.
behaviors.justification: Line-number behavior comes from the real YAML parser and error-wrapping path. Mocking parser exceptions can easily hide formatting or propagation bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Provide malformed YAML in a temp config file (with known failing line), run loader, assert failure message contains that line number from parser output.

behaviors.description: If config directory is missing at startup, loader creates it and writes defaults before continuing.
behaviors.justification: This is filesystem side-effect behavior across startup flow. In-process mocks of file APIs can miss real path creation/write ordering and existence checks.
behaviors.test_description: Point config root to a fresh temp location with no directory present; run startup loader; assert directory is created, defaults file is written with expected baseline keys, and load then proceeds successfully.
